title: Fasting status documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports medical-record review, patient interview, verification of fasting status, and documentation of compliance.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration
source.population: Healthy patients of all ages undergoing elective procedures requiring anesthesia or sedation, with stated modifying conditions.
source.setting: Preprocedure fasting assessment for elective anesthesia or sedation.
source.exclusions: Exact for preoperative record review, interview, fasting compliance, and aspiration-risk modifiers. Fasting durations, medicine instructions, and management are not mapped.

exact_location.heading: Preoperative assessment of fasting status and compliance

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports medical-record review, patient interview, verification of fasting status, and documentation of compliance.

assessment.text: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Fasting status documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.
